serving.natural_language_portion: A standard 320 ml can of Coca-Cola, the most common single-serve portion available in Singapore retail and food service outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated beverage, cola, with caffeine
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values primarily sourced from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 174926) for 'Carbonated beverage, cola, with caffeine'. This provides a comprehensive nutrient profile and is an approved global source. HPB Singapore Foodo was used for initial cross-reference; USDA is prioritized for completeness and compliance.

gi_source.title: Soft drink, Coca-Cola — Glycemic Index Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 for Coca-Cola (regular) is documented in the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). GL per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 63 × 33.15 g (320 g × 10.36 g/100 g) / 100 = 20.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently raises blood pressure risk via insulin-mediated sodium retention and weight gain, per AHA 2018 guidelines.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Coca-Cola entirely; the 9.94 g/100 g sugar load in liquid form drives fructose-mediated uric acid production and sodium retention, and the caffeine content provides additional acute pressor effects — replace with water or unsweetened sparkling water.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9.94 g sugar/100 g and zero dietary fibre, causing rapid postprandial glucose and insulin spikes with no nutritional modulation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Coca-Cola entirely; a 320 ml can delivers approximately 31.8 g of rapidly absorbed sugar with a glycemic load of 20.8, producing acute hyperglycaemia — switch to water, unsweetened sparkling water, or diet variants only under medical guidance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk through dyslipidaemia, weight gain, and elevated triglycerides driven by fructose metabolism.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should eliminate Coca-Cola from their diet; habitual SSB consumption raises triglycerides and promotes visceral adiposity — the phosphoric acid content may additionally impair calcium absorption relevant to vascular calcification risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) with zero anti-inflammatory nutrients promotes systemic inflammatory mediators; no omega-3, polyphenols, fibre, or antioxidants present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Coca-Cola entirely; the high fructose corn syrup content promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity, and caffeine may interact with RA medications — consult prescribing physician.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coca-Cola is a globally recognised carbonated cola soft drink manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company, originally created in the United States in 1886. It is an ultra-processed, sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) composed primarily of carbonated water, high fructose corn syrup (or sucrose depending on market), caramel colour (Class IV), phosphoric acid, natural flavours, and caffeine. The drink is characterised by its distinctive sweet-acidic cola flavour, dark brown colour, and effervescent mouthfeel. Per 100 g, it provides 42 kcal, 10.36 g carbohydrate (of which 9.94 g is sugar), negligible protein (0 g by formulation), and negligible fat (0 g by formulation). Sodium content is low at 3 mg/100 g; however, the high sugar load independently elevates cardiovascular and metabolic risk. Coca-Cola has a glycemic index of 63 (medium) and a glycemic load of approximately 20.8 per 320 ml can, making it clinically significant for blood glucose management. It is widely available in Singapore in 320 ml cans and 500 ml bottles. Clinically, Coca-Cola is contraindicated for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sugar content, absence of fibre, and pro-inflammatory profile. It is classified as halal, vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free. The product is sold globally and is one of the most consumed beverages worldwide.
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in a can of Coke?
Can I drink Coke if I have high blood pressure?
What is the glycemic index of Coca-Cola?
How many calories in a 320ml can of Coke?
What are the ingredients in Coca-Cola?
Is Coke bad for rheumatoid arthritis?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values primarily sourced from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 174926) for 'Carbonated beverage, cola, with caffeine', which is an exact match and approved source. GI value of 63 sourced from University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). All core macros and secondary nutrients are supported by named approved sources.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — Coca-Cola contains no protein-bearing ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 174926). fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — Coca-Cola contains no fat-bearing ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a carbonated sugar-sweetened beverage with no fibre-containing ingredients. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero as this is a plant-derived beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to 0.0 as confirmed by USDA FoodData Central for standard cola. trans_fat_g set to 0.0 as confirmed by USDA. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are confirmed zero by USDA FoodData Central for 'Carbonated beverage, cola, with caffeine' (FDC ID 174926), indicating these nutrients are not present in significant amounts. phosphorus_mg (10.0 mg) and water_g (89.0 g) are also from USDA FoodData Central. Input cultural.primary_cuisine was listed as 'Korean' which is incorrect for Coca-Cola — this is a globally manufactured American product; cuisine corrected to 'Global / American' and region_of_origin set to 'United States'. Korean local name (코카콜라) retained as the product is widely sold in Korea and the Korean name is the standard transliteration. Two default portions flagged in input (both is_default: true) — retained both as common serving sizes for Singapore market (320 ml can, 500 ml bottle). GL calculation: GI (63) × carbohydrate per 320 g serving (33.15 g) / 100 = 20.8.
